# **Video of the day: Safety before politics**

---
| **This evening,** President Donald J. Trump called on Democrats to come together and put the safety of the American people before politics.

“Border security must become a #1 priority!” the President tweeted.
---
|
---
|
## **The 2018 Farm Bill, explained**

**A few hours ago,** President Trump signed into law one of his biggest priorities for 2018: a Farm Bill that will keep American agriculture and farmers on top globally.

**For the first time since 1990** , a farm bill has been enacted in the same year it was introduced before Congress. The 2018 bill provides crucial funding for the U.S. Department of Agriculture, as well as support, certainty, and stability for America’s farmers. Specifically, the new law:
* Extends farm support programs and improves crop insurance

* Opens new markets for American farmers

* Maintains vital disaster programs

* Promotes voluntary conservation

**The 2018 Farm Bill,** which also expands rural broadband access and dedicates resources to fighting the opioid crisis, is just the latest in President Trump’s mission to put rural communities back on track. Saturday will mark the one-year anniversary of the President’s historic Tax Cuts and Jobs Act, for example, which expanded the estate tax exemption that is so crucial to family farms across America.

**Of course, nothing is perfect**. Although the Farm Bill enhanced Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) program integrity, it missed an opportunity to close loopholes that trap Americans in poverty. To help fix that, the Administration is proposing a new rule with commonsense measures to move SNAP recipients toward self-sufficiency through meaningful work whenever possible.

[President Trump signs the Farm Bill: What you should know](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=5579c59de0&e=3ee4985eea)

**Throwback Thursday** : [President Trump, _Green Acres_ style!](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=ba5bb54bde&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
|
## **Big news on border security**

**Aliens trying to enter the United States** to claim asylum will no longer be released into our country, where they often disappear before an immigration judge can determine their claim’s merits.

**Instead, these individuals** will be processed by the Department of Homeland Security and given a “Notice to Appear” date. The Mexican government has made its own determination to provide humanitarian visas, work authorization, and other protections while these aliens await their hearing in a U.S. immigration court. They will also have access to immigration attorneys.

**Those whose claims** are upheld by U.S. judges will be allowed to enter the United States. Those without valid claims will be deported to their home countries.

[Secretary Nielsen announces historic action to confront illegal immigration.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=4f23e9f251&e=3ee4985eea)

name: Molly Kelly
subject: Wishing you a joyful season
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Supporters of Molly Kelly
body:
[](https://mollykelly.bsd.net/page/m/70fc4afb/d0ce7de/5087db11/9cdf05e/4229489094/VEsH/ "http://www.mollykelly.com")
[](https://mollykelly.bsd.net/page/m/70fc4afb/d0ce7de/5087db11/9cdf05e/4229489094/VEsE/ "http://www.mollykelly.com")
Friends,
With the holidays upon us, I wanted to wish you a season filled with joy and time with family and friends, as well as a new year of hope and peace.
I'm grateful for all of your support throughout 2018. Yes, we may have come up short, but I'm proud of our campaign - which would not have been possible without you.
With a new Democratic majority in the state legislature and on the Executive Council, we are well positioned to build a New Hampshire that works for everyone. That means providing job training for workers, passing common sense gun safety and making paid family and medical leave a reality.
I will continue to fight for the values and beliefs that we share and I look forward to continuing to work with you in 2019 and beyond.
From the bottom of my heart, thank you again.
Molly

body:
| | Alveda King’s thoughts on the Christmas Season.
---
[ ](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6_nuz-gPbh4AR2HufX66icYcIyRhB2URpXghNXmPGvMssmdXqrrthtpNd1UrG9ctuc7ixZfgI0ZWPu65N6RAzkPBRGb87Iv8cAqQYpGic8M&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e181221com01&n=1)
| It’s hard to believe Christmas is just 4 days away. Before you disconnect from your daily responsibilities and begin spending quality time with family and friends this next week, read evangelist Alveda King’s thoughts on how to have a truly happy Christmas season between now and the New Year. Alveda’s **[article](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6_nuz-gPbh4AR2HufX66icYcIyRhB2URpXghNXmPGvMssmdXqrrthtpNd1UrG9ctuc7ixZfgI0ZWPu65N6RAzkPBRGb87Iv8cAqQYpGic8M&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e181221com01&n=2)** reminds us of the opportunities we have to share the love of Christ with the people the Lord has placed in our lives. “Around Thanksgiving Season, I begin to half-jokingly say: ‘Don’t be guilty of ripping the leg off the turkey and beating up others with it.’ I only say half-jokingly because so many families are divided these days. But does strife have to happen in our families? During the Christmas season, there will be plenty of good food to go around. In addition to spending time with family and friends, our ministry always joins in efforts to feed the hungry. Then, for the rest of the day, our family makes the rounds from house to house, delivering delicious dishes and good cheer. All of this is good; but without Agape Love at the tables, something is missing. Along with genuine Agape Love, another addition to the meals and festivities should be a ‘spiritual fruit bowl.’” [](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6_nuz-gPbh4AR2HufX66icYcIyRhB2URpXghNXmPGvMssmdXqrrthtpNd1UrG9ctuc7ixZfgI0ZWPu65N6RAzkPBRGb87Iv8cAqQYpGic8M&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e181221com01&n=3)

Mary

subject: DHS to Make Asylum-Seekers Wait in Mexico in Attempt to End 'Catch-and-Release'
body:
Asylum-seekers crossing Mexico to reach the United States will now have to wait for an immigration court hearing before being granted access... | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=dd53889b8b&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **President Trump Signs $867 Billion Farm Bill, Tightening Rules for Food Stamp Recipients**

In Fox News, Paulina Dedaj reports that President Donald J. Trump signed a bipartisan 2018 Farm Bill yesterday that secures more funding to put U.S. agriculture and American farmers on top.

“We are ensuring that American agriculture will always feed our families, nourish our communities, power our commerce and inspire our nation,” the President said. The bill will improve crop insurance, disaster relief programs, and access to rural broadband, among other provisions.

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=755ed12b37&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| Asylum-seekers crossing Mexico to reach the United States will now have to wait for an immigration court hearing before being granted access to the American homeland, Stephen Dinan reports for _[The Washington Times](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=c25377c4d3&e=3ee4985eea)_. “Homeland Security Secretary Kirstjen Nielsen said the goal is to end the ‘catch and release’ practice that has enticed a wave of illegal immigrants to head north in recent years, convinced that they will be able to make bogus asylum claims and then slip into the shadows.”
---
|
---
| “The House voted Thursday to give President Trump $5.7 billion for a border wall, hours after Trump warned Republicans that he would veto the spending bill if it didn't boost border security,” Susan Ferrechio reports in the _[Washington Examiner](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=82a5e10621&e=3ee4985eea)_. President Trump met with Republicans at the White House yesterday to make his position clear. The bill now moves to the Senate for a vote today.
---
|
---
| “Congress approved a sweeping bipartisan criminal justice reform bill on Thursday, handing President Trump a major legislative victory on an issue championed by his White House,” Adam Shaw writes for [Fox News](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=3ac2b2487c&e=3ee4985eea). “This is a great bi-partisan achievement for everybody,” President Trump tweeted. “When both parties work together we can keep our Country safer.”
---

name: Tina Smith
subject: My experience at the border
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tina Smith for Minnesota
body:
Last weekend I took a trip to the Mexican border with Mazie Hirono, Jeff Merkley, Judy Chu, and Beto O'Rourke.

| [](https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/m/4bc4d2fe/c1f7015/61c57724/1140c155/2067451182/VEsH/ "https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/s/rtw_201812_tornillo?source=rtw_20181221_em") Dear friend, I know you're probably all set to spend some time with your loved ones, but before you take off for the holidays, there is something very important I needed to write you about. Last weekend I took a trip to the Mexican border with Mazie Hirono, Jeff Merkley, Judy Chu, and Beto O'Rourke. I wasn't sure what to expect. This summer, America and the world rose up to condemn the inhumane practice of separating children from their parents at the border as part of Trump's "zero tolerance" policy -- the same practice a federal judge declared illegal. I wanted to see for myself what this administration is doing, and what I learned was heartbreaking Our country is currently detaining 15,000 children between the ages of about 13 and 17 in detention camps. I saw about 2,700 of these children at Tornillo, a tent city in a border town about an hour outside El Paso, Texas. Most of these children came to the border without an adult, seeking help, and hoping to escape from violence in their home countries. Nearly all of them have family and friends in the United States that are ready and willing to take them as sponsors, and we continue to hold them in what are essentially child prisons -- they sleep in tents on the desert and are walked around in single file. **[This is no way to treat children. We are better than this as a country. This flies in the face of our country's founding principles. If you agree, then I urge you to act now and speak out. Your voice is strong, but only if you use it. Demand Trump and Congress close Tornillo now.](https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/m/4bc4d2fe/c1f7015/61c57724/1140c155/2067451182/VEsE/ "https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/s/rtw_201812_tornillo?source=rtw_20181221_em")** [Add Your Name](https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/m/4bc4d2fe/c1f7015/61c57724/1140c155/2067451182/VEsF/ "https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/s/rtw_201812_tornillo?source=rtw_20181221_em") The Trump administration calls these "residences." Residences are where people live. These places are jails. And every single child in these jails will have to live with this experience for the rest of their life. It's just wrong and we have to do everything we can to stop it. Please speak out now. Thank you, Tina
---

name: Sherrod Brown
subject: Trump's Shutdown
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Sherrod Brown
body:
|
---

| | While children at the border are still separated from their families over the holidays, Trump is throwing a temper tantrum about the wall. Peter if President Trump doesn't sign a deal to fund the government by midnight, about a third of the government will shut down. Trump even said he'd be "proud" of shutting the government down if he doesn't get his way. His strategy is despicable -- not to mention more than a little juvenile. Nobody wants a shutdown besides the President. A significant number of my colleagues across the aisle are ready to keep the government open. The Senate passed a bill unanimously. [**We need all hands on deck to stop this irresponsible behavior. Add your name to demand Trump keep the government open.**](https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/page/m/302ffdde/4462dc26/888b22a8/201efcaa/3381718883/VEsH/ "https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/e181221m") Trump knows exactly what he is doing, Peter. He is attempting to demonize immigrants and divide Americans. Why? He wants to distract from the fact that Wall Street is raking in millions from his tax handouts while working families still haven't seen the raises he promised. He wants to distract from the fact that his goal of repealing the Affordable Care Act will be devastating and affect nearly every American. Trump wants to distract from the fact that he is running a chaotic administration that leaves millions of Americans feeling uncertain and unsafe. He's certainly distracting the public from the troubling news that Defense Secretary James Mattis resigned. Peter, he will do anything he can to distract the American people from his failed policies. The problem is that every time Trump throws a tantrum, families pay the consequences. Americans deserve better. [**Make your voice heard. name: Senator Chris Van Hollen
subject: Senate Update: Fighting Against the Shutdown Clock
body:
| | | [Click here to open this e-mail in its own browser window](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet//view_newsletter_setup.aspx?link=SenVanHollen%2501137271%2501December%2bNewsletter%250115369.12682740.4128911.7517223%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Friend%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Silver%2bSpring%252c%2bMD%2b20906%2501) | [Click here to open a plain text version of this email](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet//view_newsletter_setup.aspx?plain_text=Y&link=SenVanHollen%2501137271%2501December%2bNewsletter%250115369.12682740.4128911.7517223%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Friend%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Silver%2bSpring%252c%2bMD%2b20906%2501)
---|---
| | | **December 21, 2018** |
---|---|---|---
| | | Today, we are on the brink of an unnecessary and reckless partial government shutdown. Earlier this week, the Senate passed a bipartisan agreement to prevent such a shutdown. While far from perfect, it would have avoided the havoc a shutdown wreaks on our country and hardworking federal employees. Then, yesterday, we watched as President Trump pulled the rug out from under the agreement after he was criticized by conservative talk show hosts. I was then deeply frustrated to see Speaker Paul Ryan and House Republicans do the President's bidding instead of letting democracy work its will and allowing the House to vote on the Senate compromise to fund the government.

This argument is not about border security. There is strong bipartisan support for securing our borders. This is about wasting $5 billion on an ineffective, 2,000-mile-long border wall that President Trump said Mexico -- not American taxpayers -- would pay for. The short-term budget agreement the Senate passed earlier this week maintains the significant funding for border security that the Congress passed last year -- much of which the President has yet to put to use. But it's clear that this common-sense approach is not sufficient for the President, who said he would be "proud" to shut down the government if he didn't get everything his way.

I will continue fighting to keep the government open. A shutdown will have dire consequences on our state and country -- shuttering large portions of agencies ranging from the Department of Justice to the Department of Agriculture to Homeland Security. The closings negatively impact small businesses, farmers, homeowners, and more. Over 26,000 jobs in Maryland could be directly impacted.

If the government does shut down, hardworking federal employees should not be punished for political dysfunction they had nothing to do with -- they should at the very least be provided the comfort of knowing that they will receive full retroactive pay once the government reopens. Senator Cardin and I have introduced legislation to do just that, and I'm very pleased to report that we passed it out of the Senate today. Now the House should pass it immediately.

As we move forward, I will keep urging my colleagues to put party politics aside to fund our government. Chris Van Hollen
---
### **Protecting Americans ' Health Care**
Since day one, President Trump and Congressional Republicans have worked to repeal, sabotage, and dismantle the Affordable Care Act. Each time, we stood together and refused to stay silent because we knew what was at stake. Without this landmark law, tens of millions of Americans, including many of our friends, neighbors, and family members, would be left without the lifesaving care they need. Healthcare costs would become unaffordable for millions more, and people would be left without important protections.

Unfortunately, we must unite once again to defend our healthcare system. Last week, a federal judge in Texas -- after pressure from Congressional Republicans, the Trump Administration, and Republican Attorneys General -- ruled that the Affordable Care Act is unconstitutional. If upheld, this ruling will have a devastating impact on our entire healthcare system. It will threaten access to coverage and protections -- including for those with pre-existing conditions -- for millions of people, potentially leaving them with astronomically high costs or no healthcare coverage at all.

As this ruling makes its way through the courts, the President and the GOP are trying to accomplish in court what they couldn't do in Congress. I will continue to use every tool at my disposal to fight against this effort -- and I urge you to do the same. This week I introduced a bill with some of my Senate colleagues authorizing the Senate Legal Counsel to represent the Senate in court to defend all provisions of the ACA. I'm confident the new House will authorize their legal counsel to do the same come January.
### Supporting Farmers, the **Chesapeake Bay******, and Our Environment ********
Last week, Congress passed a bipartisan five-year Farm Bill that authorizes and updates programs that help our farmers and protect our environment. It includes important wins for our state, and we defeated attempts to undermine conservation efforts.

I'm pleased to announce that the legislation includes three major initiatives that I've championed in the Senate. First, the Farm Bill includes my plan to significantly increase funding for the Regional Conservation Partnership Program -- a program that helps farmers and conservation groups work together to reduce runoff and prevent pollution. It also makes improvements to the program so that increased funding goes to sensitive environmental areas like the Chesapeake Bay watershed.

The bill also contains provisions I introduced to reauthorize and improve access to assistance for veteran and socially disadvantaged farmers, as well as language I introduced to correct an inequity and give 1890s Land-Grant Universities (which are all Historically Black Colleges and Universities) the same flexibility to use their funding as other Land Grant universities. Both of these programs will help the University of Maryland Eastern Shore and the great work it's doing in our state.
### Improving Justice for All
For all the understandable cynicism about Congress being unable to accomplish anything significant, there was a little ray of light here as we head into the New Year: the Congress took an important first step to reform our broken criminal justice system. The First Step Act is backed by a diverse group of supporters -- from the American Civil Liberties Union to the Fraternal Order of Police. It includes crucial reforms to reduce mandatory minimum sentences for non-violent crimes, end juvenile solitary confinement, and provide more people with a second chance.

As the bill makes clear, this is just the beginning of the work we need to do to ensure that all Americans have equal rights and equal justice under the law. For example, I've partnered with my friend Senator Cory Booker to push to end private prisons, which have seen countless abuses and create perverse profit incentives to lock up more people for longer periods of time. I also believe Maryland has taken several important steps, including on bail reform, that the federal government should examine on a national basis. And through my role on the Appropriations Committee, I'll keep fighting to secure critical resources for programs that improve our justice system, such as the Justice Reinvestment Initiative and the Law Enforcement Assisted Diversion program.

America's history has always been tied to the struggle for equal rights and opportunity for all. Working together, we will keep fighting to build an ever more perfect union.

body:
We're just days away from Christmas, so I'll keep this short. Besides, you you don't need any more politics than you'll get at your dinner table... | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/indiana/christmas-came-early?e=1acd9bb3d6)
---
| |
---
|
---
| **_Christmas Came Early_** We're just days away from Christmas, so I'll keep this short. If you're like me, you'll get plenty of politics at the family dinner table this holiday season, so you don't need any more from me now.

Together, we've had a remarkable 2018, and really, Christmas came early. **Senator-elect Mike Braun** defeated Democrat Joe Donnelly in the top targeted U.S. Senate race in the nation. And that's while we also re-elected our statewide, all-female Republican team of Secretary of **State Connie Lawson, Auditor Tera Klutz** and **Treasurer Kelly Mitchell. **

But it doesn't stop there. With your vote, this year we also maintained our Republican supermajories in the Indiana House and Senate, while we passed a balanced budget amendment to the Indiana Constitution. This means that future generations of Hoosiers will have the assurance that we have now -- knowing that our leaders will be fiscally responsible and live within our means.

It's been a remarkable year. Merry Christmas, Hoosiers! -Kyle Hupfer
---
|
---

| **_Another Record Year!_** There's never been a better time to be a Hoosier!! That's because Indiana's economy isn't just hopping now (private sector employment just [PEAKED in Indiana](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=ea5367a10b&e=1acd9bb3d6)!; through**Governor Eric Holcomb's** leadership, it'll continue to grow well into the future.

This week, **Governor Holcomb** [helped announce a second record year for job commitments](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=ef1d54f99d&e=1acd9bb3d6) from the Indiana Economic Development Corporation! And it paints a great picture for the future of Indiana...with more than 31,000 high-wage jobs on their way to the state in the next few years. Check out the numbers in the graphic above!

**Governor Holcomb** said it best himself: “I’m thrilled to announce back-to-back record years for new job commitments as we work every day to ensure that more Hoosiers have the opportunity to grow and thrive here in Indiana...While the numbers speak for themselves, the long-term career opportunities that come with them are even more meaningful.”

subject: Third time's a charm? (shutdown)

body:
|

| | In August 2017, I began an email to you all with these words: **Donald Trump said he would refuse to sign a bill that doesn't force American taxpayers to fund his absurd border wall.** Sound familiar? This time, Trump wants $5 billion for his border monstrosity. And if he doesn't get it, he said he'd be "proud to shut down the government" -- leaving hundreds of thousands of federal employees without a paycheck, just in time for the holidays. [**Demand Trump keep the government open. Add your name >>**](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113ccf/622b7b5b/1e7df2c2/307b7725/2569198898/VEsH/ "https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/e181219ama") This would be the third time the government shut down since Trump took office. These shutdowns don't help people. They hurt our families and our communities. Thousands will be sent home. Over 400,000 "essential" federal workers will be forced to work with no idea when (or even if) they'll be compensated. Lots of people travel this time of year. If the government shuts down, TSA agents will be working without pay. So would air traffic controllers. Even the Secret Service agents protecting the president and his family would be working without pay if Trump shuts down the government. You would think after shutting down the government twice before, disrupting millions of families and many vital services, Trump would understand that he cannot extort billions of dollars out of Congress to waste on his immoral and ultimately ineffective border wall. But I suppose we'll just have to show him yet again. **No one except Trump wants the government to shut down over Trump's border wall.[If you agree, add your name.](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113ccf/622b7b5b/1e7df2c2/307b7725/2569198898/VEsE/ "https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/e181219ama")** Thank you. Debbie

name: Jay Inslee
subject: It has been a consistent downward spiral
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Inslee for Washington
body:
President Trump's con is up.

| [](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7affe/2f5ea88d/7a18c5e2/2cbdd638/988480041/VEsH/ "https://twitter.com/AM2DM/status/1076138545322983424") Friend -- This is a predictable consequence of Trump's narcissistic behavior. And it's up to every patriotic American -- of both parties -- to end this downward spiral. Jim Mattis is out as the Secretary of Defense. Our government is headed towards a totally avoidable shutdown. President Trump is running our country like a game of Monopoly, and he's failing the American people in the process. Look, this is the time of year to spend with family and friends. It's a time that should be about relaxation and rejuvenation after a tough year. But President Trump has once again forced us into chaos so we must make this a moment of reckoning. [**Today, I joined BuzzFeed News to talk about what 's going on and the consequences of President Trump's failed leadership. Give it a watch if you have a moment.**](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7affe/2f5ea88d/7a18c5e2/2cbdd638/988480041/VEsE/ "https://twitter.com/AM2DM/status/1076138545322983424") [](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7affe/2f5ea88d/7a18c5e2/2cbdd638/988480041/VEsF/ "https://twitter.com/AM2DM/status/1076138545322983424") Donald Trump has built his presidency around a consistent pattern of chaos. Sadly, this has been predictable from day one. President Trump's con is up. And the fight to rescue America begins anew in 2019! Very truly yours, Jay Inslee | [WATCH NOW »](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7affe/2f5ea88d/7a18c5e2/2cbdd638/988480041/VEsC/ "https://twitter.com/AM2DM/status/1076138545322983424")
